name: Utpanna Ekadashi
description: Marks the appearance of Ekadashi Devi and is traditionally connected with the origin of Ekadashi vrata.

name: Mokshada Ekadashi
description: Associated with liberation and with the day Sri Krishna spoke Bhagavad-gita to Arjuna.

name: Saphala Ekadashi
description: Observed for spiritual success and purification through devotion and restraint.

name: Putrada Ekadashi
description: Traditionally observed with prayers for blessings, continuity, and spiritual welfare of family.

name: Shat-tila Ekadashi
description: Connected with charity, austerity, and the six traditional uses of sesame.

name: Jaya Ekadashi
description: Observed for victory over lower tendencies and progress in devotional life.

name: Vijaya Ekadashi
description: Known as an Ekadashi of victory, traditionally linked with success in righteous endeavors.

name: Amalaki Ekadashi
description: Associated with worship of Lord Vishnu and the sacred amalaki tree.

name: Papamochani Ekadashi
description: Observed for release from sinful reactions and renewed spiritual discipline.

name: Kamada Ekadashi
description: Known as an Ekadashi that fulfills spiritual desires when observed with devotion.

name: Varuthini Ekadashi
description: Associated with protection, purification, and devotional merit.

name: Mohini Ekadashi
description: Connected with Lord Vishnu's Mohini form and liberation from illusion.

name: Apara Ekadashi
description: Observed for great devotional merit and removal of accumulated faults.

name: Nirjala Ekadashi
description: A strict Ekadashi fast traditionally observed without water by those who are able.

name: Yogini Ekadashi
description: Observed for purification and relief from offenses through devotion.

name: Devashayani Ekadashi
description: Begins the Chaturmasya period and marks Lord Vishnu's divine rest.

name: Kamika Ekadashi
description: Emphasizes worship of Lord Vishnu and the purifying power of sincere devotion.

name: Pavitropana Ekadashi
description: Associated with offering pavitra and renewing devotional commitments.

name: Annada Ekadashi
description: Known for removing distress and restoring spiritual fortune.

name: Parivartini Ekadashi
description: Marks Lord Vishnu turning in His divine sleep during Chaturmasya.

name: Indira Ekadashi
description: Observed for benefit of ancestors and devotional upliftment.

name: Pasankusha Ekadashi
description: Associated with worship of Lord Padmanabha and spiritual purification.

name: Rama Ekadashi
description: Observed during Kartika with special emphasis on devotion and purification.

name: Prabodhini Ekadashi
description: Marks Lord Vishnu's awakening and the conclusion of Chaturmasya.

name: Padmini Ekadashi
description: The Gaura Ekadashi of Adhika or Purushottama Masa.

name: Parama Ekadashi
description: The Krishna Ekadashi of Adhika or Purushottama Masa.
